sample.claimText: Calcimycin is defined by NIH MeSH as: An ionophorous, polyether antibiotic from Streptomyces chartreusensis. It binds and transports CALCIUM and other divalent cations across membranes and uncouples oxidative phosphorylation while inhibiting ATPase of rat liver mitochondria. The substance is used mostly as a biochemical tool to study the role of divalent cations in various biological systems.
Temefos is defined by NIH MeSH as: An organothiophosphate insecticide.
Abattoirs is defined by NIH MeSH as: Places where animals are slaughtered and dressed for market.
Abbreviations as Topic is defined by NIH MeSH as: Works about shortened forms of written words or phrases used for brevity.
Abdomen is defined by NIH MeSH as: That portion of the body that lies between the THORAX and the PELVIS.

Abdomen, Acute is defined by NIH MeSH as: A clinical syndrome with acute abdominal pain that is severe, localized, and rapid in onset. Acute abdomen may be caused by a variety of disorders, injuries, or diseases.
Abdominal Injuries is defined by NIH MeSH as: General or unspecified injuries involving organs in the abdominal cavity.
Abdominal Neoplasms is defined by NIH MeSH as: New abnormal growth of tissue in the ABDOMEN.

Abdominal Muscles is defined by NIH MeSH as: Muscles forming the ABDOMINAL WALL including RECTUS ABDOMINIS; ABDOMINAL OBLIQUE MUSCLES, transversus abdominis, pyramidalis muscles and quadratus abdominis.

Abducens Nerve is defined by NIH MeSH as: The 6th cranial nerve which originates in the ABDUCENS NUCLEUS of the PONS and sends motor fibers to the lateral rectus muscles of the EYE. Damage to the nerve or its nucleus disrupts horizontal eye movement control.

Abelson murine leukemia virus is defined by NIH MeSH as: A replication-defective strain of Murine leukemia virus (LEUKEMIA VIRUS, MURINE) capable of transforming lymphoid cells and producing a rapidly progressing lymphoid leukemia after superinfection with FRIEND MURINE LEUKEMIA VIRUS; MOLONEY MURINE LEUKEMIA VIRUS; or RAUSCHER VIRUS.

Abetalipoproteinemia is defined by NIH MeSH as: An autosomal recessive disorder of lipid metabolism. It is caused by mutation of the microsomal triglyceride transfer protein that catalyzes the transport of lipids (TRIGLYCERIDES; CHOLESTEROL ESTERS; PHOSPHOLIPIDS) and is required in the secretion of BETA-LIPOPROTEINS (low density lipoproteins or LDL). Features include defective intestinal lipid absorption, very low serum cholesterol level, and near absent LDL.
Congenital Abnormalities is defined by NIH MeSH as: Malformations of organs or body parts during development in utero.

Abnormalities, Drug-Induced is defined by NIH MeSH as: Congenital abnormalities caused by medicinal substances or drugs of abuse given to or taken by the mother, or to which she is inadvertently exposed during the manufacture of such substances. The concept excludes abnormalities resulting from exposure to non-medicinal chemicals in the environment.
Abnormalities, Multiple is defined by NIH MeSH as: Congenital abnormalities that affect more than one organ or body structure.
